name: Route of the Mills of the Perales River
name_original: Ruta de los molinos — circular desde Navalagamella
description: Hike the moderate 4.6-mile Route of the mills circular from Navalagamella, exploring historical mills, oak forests, and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Navalagamella offers diverse natural landscapes within the Community of Madrid. The region features dense oak forests, particularly in granitic areas, with ash trees and black poplars in damper zones. The Perales River is a central feature, providing scenic views along its banks, while the Cerro Alarcón Reservoir offers tranquil waterside paths. The terrain includes various streams and arroyos, contributing to a varied hiking environment. Best hiking trails around Navalagamella The most popular hiking route is Route of the Mills of the Perales River, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This path leads past historic mills along the Perales River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro Alarcón Reservoir Loop, an easy 3.8 miles (6.1 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir, providing a tranquil setting for walks. Local hikers also love the Puente del Hoyo – Puente del Pasadero loop from Navalagamella, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical bridges, often completed in about 4 hours 11 minutes. Hiking around Navalagamella is defined by oak forests, the Perales River, and historical sites like ancient mills and Civil War bunkers. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to moderate loops. The routes in Navalagamella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 450 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Navalagamella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Navalagamella?
A: Navalagamella offers diverse natural landscapes, including dense oak forests, especially in its granitic areas. You'll also find ash trees and black poplars in damper zones, and scenic views along the Perales River. The Cerro Alarcón Reservoir provides tranquil waterside paths, and the terrain is crisscrossed by various streams and arroyos.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, Navalagamella's trails often feature historical elements. You can explore ancient mills along the Perales River, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375833431/route-of-the-mills-of-the-perales-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills of the Perales River&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical structures include the restored Puente del Hoyo and the medieval Puente del Pasadero. Some routes also pass by remnants from the Spanish Civil War, like bunkers and fortresses.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hiking trails in Navalagamella?
A: The hiking trails in Navalagamella cater to various fitness levels. There are 9 easy routes and 7 moderate routes available. You won't find any difficult trails, making it suitable for a wide range of hikers looking for accessible outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Navalagamella?
A: Yes, many routes in Navalagamella are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375862833/cerro-alarcon-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Alarcón Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile circular path offering tranquil views. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6928339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Hoyo – Puente del Pasadero loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 9.4 miles long.

Q: What kind of wildlife or unique flora might I encounter?
A: The region is rich in flora, characterized by dense oak forests, ash trees, and black poplars. Along the Perales River, you'll find riparian ecosystems. A remarkable centuries-old juniper tree, estimated to be between 300 and 400 years old, is also a notable natural feature that can be encountered on certain trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Navalagamella?
A: Yes, Navalagamella has several family-friendly options. Many of the 9 easy routes are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375833431/route-of-the-mills-of-the-perales-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Mills of the Perales River&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is an easy 3.2-mile path that takes about 1 hour 26 minutes to complete, making it a good choice for outings with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Navalagamella?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail are not provided, generally, trails in natural areas like Navalagamella are dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage at trailheads for any specific restrictions or requirements regarding pets.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navalagamella?
A: The trails in Navalagamella are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 450 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical points of interest, and the well-maintained paths suitable for various abilities.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs to visit near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant body of water nearby, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375862833/cerro-alarcon-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Alarcón Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers picturesque views and tranquil waterside paths directly within Navalagamella.

Q: What are some other natural attractions or viewpoints in the vicinity?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several natural monuments and viewpoints. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6556720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bear Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4219128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Fraile Rock Formation&lt;/a&gt;. There's also a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6277005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone slab above the Valmayor reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offering scenic views.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking areas in Navalagamella?
A: Navalagamella is accessible by public transport, typically bus services from Madrid. It's advisable to check the latest bus schedules and routes to plan your journey to the starting points of the hiking trails.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting the trails?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the town of Navalagamella, especially near popular trailheads or the town center. Specific parking areas for individual routes are usually indicated on local maps or trail descriptions.
tips: In the town of Navalagamella you can see this Herrerian-style church that dates back to the late 15th century and early 16th century, classified as an Asset of Cultural Interest (BIC).

The area you are in was of great importance during the Spanish Civil War, and it is still possible to see many remains of it today. To the east of Navalagamella you can find these bunker forts that belonged to the Franco army and were built after the battle of Brunete.

Within the ecological route of the mills, this is one of them, although if you continue north, you will be able to see more. Some of them, like the Molino Alto, are in a much better state of conservation.
This mill is within the Navalagamella mills route. In good condition, it has an explanatory panel where you can understand what it was like and how it worked.

This bridge that passes over the Perales River has two rectangular abutments and a central pier, also rectangular but with a triangular cutwater.
In 2019 it underwent restoration work.
Spectacular viewpoint to the dense oak forest with the most iconic peaks of the Sierra de Guadarrama in the background. Starting point of the ecological path of the mills.

faqs.question: What is the estimated hiking time for the Route of the mills — circular from Navalagamella?
faqs.answer: The estimated hiking time for this route is approximately 2 hours and 3 minutes, based on its 7.3 km distance and moderate difficulty. This duration allows for enjoying the scenery and points of interest.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a variety of historical and natural sights. You can explore ancient 'molinos de Cubo' (cube mills), visit the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Estrella, and see remnants from the Civil War, such as the Calavera Bunker Position (Navalagamella). Don't miss the Molino de Baltasar, Molino Alto Bridge, and Puente del Hoyo.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Route of the mills — circular from Navalagamella?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring natural paths, unpaved sections, and some paved roads and streets. You'll hike through dense oak forests and along the natural environment of the Perales River, offering diverse scenery throughout.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally accessible year-round. However, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Winter can also be beautiful, but be prepared for potentially cooler conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the mills — circular from Navalagamella dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near historical sites, and to follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Route of the mills — circular from Navalagamella?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting from Navalagamella. Parking is typically available within the town, near the starting point of the ecological path of the mills. Look for designated parking areas or street parking in Navalagamella.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of the mills — circular from Navalagamella. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Navalagamella?
faqs.answer: Yes, Navalagamella is a town with local amenities. You should be able to find cafes, restaurants, or pubs in the village center where you can refresh before or after your hike.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Cañada Real Leonesa, Camino de Valdemorillo, Colada, Abrevadero y Descansadero de Vahondillo, and Camino de las Laderas.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, the route can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. The historical elements and river environment offer engaging points of interest for younger hikers.

faqs.question: What are the best viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Mirador del Hondillo is a key viewpoint on this route, offering spectacular vistas of the dense oak forest with the iconic peaks of the Sierra de Guadarrama in the background. It also serves as a starting point for the ecological path of the mills.

waypoints.name: Church of Nuestra Señora de la Estrella
